methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuamCore is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Alon Cohen that has raised $35M to date. The valuation analysis considers a comp universe of 11 peers: three publicly traded firms (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and eight private-stage companies including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression methodology applies directly to this valuation. Using $35M in total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement yields an implied valuation of $70M. Peer-median triangulation does not support a second independent method; while five quantum computing peers exist in the broader universe, none qualify as tight comparables at the same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. Attempting to force peer multiples would introduce material estimation error.

The single applicable method supports a valuation range of $60M (low) to $80M (high), with a median point of $70M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodology rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of true same-stage peer data in quantum computing—a nascent, heterogeneous sector where financing patterns and valuations remain fragmented—and the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for this specific domain. A Series B financing round with marked institutional participation would provide clearer calibration.
